rationale: The page provides specific factual details regarding the administration, eligibility criteria, and selection process for national film awards in India, which is a relevant topic for Indian Art and Culture.
cleanedText:
• Announced by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting.
• Eligibility criteria: Films must be produced in India, with the director and makers being Indian nationals. Co-productions with foreign entities must meet specific conditions.
• Films should be certified by the Central Board of Film Certification between 1st January and 31st December each year.
• Over 100 films from across the country are entered in each category (Feature and Non-Feature).
• The juries, appointed by the Directorate of Film Festivals, have strict criteria for eligibility and are not influenced by the Government or Directorate.
